Exterior Window Cleaning in Sicamous: What You're Actually Dealing With

Exterior glass in Sicamous accumulates a layered mix of contamination that is unlike anywhere else in the Shuswap: diesel particulate from marina traffic, mineral deposits from lake spray off both Shuswap and Mara Lakes, and dust stirred by Highway 1 traffic running directly through town. Properties on the water-facing side of the marina district can develop visible haze on exterior panes within weeks of a clean during peak summer. The tourism season compresses demand sharply — most Sicamous exterior cleaning is booked between late April and mid-June as property owners prepare for arrivals. Houses along Malakwa Road farther from the water accumulate more road dust and less lake spray, meaning their exterior glass pattern is driven more by wind direction and vehicle traffic than by water proximity.

What is Exterior Window Cleaning and Why Does It Matter in Sicamous?

Exterior Window Cleaning in Sicamous involves removing the specific buildup that accumulates on glass in this area — mineral deposits from lake spray, pollen from surrounding forests, and the seasonal grime unique to the Shuswap's four-season climate. For Sicamous homeowners, clean windows aren't just cosmetic — they protect the glass surface from long-term etching and staining that becomes harder to remove over time.

Located at the junction of Shuswap and Mara Lakes, approximately 45 km east of Salmon Arm on Highway 1. Known as the 'Houseboat Capital of Canada.' This geography shapes how windows age here. Sicamous sits between two lakes — windborne moisture and mineral spray are significant. Higher tourism traffic in summer means commercial cleaning demand peaks sharply in June–August.

Between two lakes — both residential and commercial properties face heavy lake-spray mineral buildup. The calcium and silica left behind after lake water evaporates bonds to glass over time — standard cleaning won't remove it without the right treatment. Early intervention is always cheaper than remediation.

Professional exterior addresses these challenges systematically. Where DIY cleaning smears mineral deposits or misses the frame and sill buildup, a proper clean removes every layer — leaving glass that's genuinely clear rather than just less dirty.

For Sicamous's mix of residential homes, vacation rentals, waterfront properties, and commercial tourism businesses (marinas, hotels, restaurants)., the investment in professional cleaning pays off both in appearance and in the long-term health of the glass itself.

What's Included in Our Exterior Window Cleaning in Sicamous

Our exterior in Sicamous is a complete clean from frame to sill — not just a wipe of the glass. Every job includes our standard process adapted to the specific conditions of your property, with a streak-free guarantee on every pane.

  • All exterior-facing windows cleaned
  • Squeegee + detailing for streak-free finish
  • Sill and frame wipe-down
  • Hard-to-reach windows with proper extension equipment
  • Eco-friendly cleaning solution — safe for gardens and pets

For lakefront and lake-view properties in Sicamous, we pay particular attention to panes facing the water — these accumulate mineral deposits faster than interior-facing windows and require more careful treatment to achieve a streak-free result.

We work around your schedule and access requirements. For properties with multiple stories or restricted access points, we discuss the approach before starting and will tell you upfront if a ladder or extension pole is needed.

Exterior Window Cleaning Pricing in Sicamous

Window cleaning in Sicamous starts from $169 for exterior-only on a small home (up to 15 windows) and from $219 for full interior + exterior. We publish our prices upfront — no mystery quotes.

Large26–35From $299From $379

Add-ons: Screen cleaning $3–$4 per screen · Track cleaning from $50 · Hard water stain removal quoted on-site · Minimum job $119

Mix of residential homes, vacation rentals, waterfront properties, and commercial tourism businesses (marinas, hotels, restaurants). Most Sicamous properties fall in the medium or large range — expect a full clean to run $199–$349 for a typical home in the area.

How We Do Exterior Window Cleaning in Sicamous: Step by Step

Every Wagon Windows job follows the same process. Here's exactly what happens from the moment we arrive at your Sicamous property.

  1. 1. Access Assessment

    Before starting, the full exterior of the property is walked to identify every accessible window, flag any obstacles — overgrown shrubs, deck furniture, low-clearance eaves — and determine which windows require extension equipment versus standard reach. This assessment ensures the job is completed safely and that no window is skipped due to an access issue discovered mid-clean.

  2. 2. Dry Debris Clear

    Loose debris on frames, sills, and screen inserts is brushed away before any liquid is applied. In Shuswap's cottonwood and wildfire smoke seasons, this dry-first step is essential — wet debris turns to paste under a squeegee and contaminates the solution, requiring full reapplication. Frames and ledges are cleared top to bottom so debris doesn't fall onto already-cleaned glass below.

  3. 3. Pre-Rinse with Clean Water

    A clean water rinse is applied to all exterior surfaces to loosen surface grime, pollen, and dust before the cleaning solution makes contact. This step reduces the concentration of contaminants in the solution and prevents clogging of the applicator, which would leave streaks. On heavily soiled windows — common after Salmon Arm's dry, dusty summers — this rinse may be repeated twice.

  4. 4. Cleaning Solution Application

    Professional window cleaning solution is loaded into a wet applicator and worked across each pane in firm, overlapping strokes to ensure full coverage. The solution emulsifies mineral deposits, insect residue, and environmental oils that bond to glass over time. On windows with visible water-scale buildup from sprinkler or lake spray contact, extra dwell time is allowed before proceeding.

  5. 5. Squeegee Finish

    Each pane is squeegeed using professional brass-channel tools with fresh rubber blades — worn blades leave lines and are replaced per job, not per season. Overlapping pull strokes remove solution and dissolved grime in a single controlled pass. The tool is wiped clean between each stroke to prevent transferring grime back onto the glass.

  6. 6. Sill & Frame Wipe-Down

    After the glass is clean, sills and frames are wiped with a damp cloth to remove any solution drips or loosened grime. This step is what separates a professional exterior clean from a DIY result — neglecting frames leaves dirty residue that runs onto the glass at the next rain and undoes the clean. All sills are left dry and streak-free.

  7. 7. Final Exterior Inspection

    A final walk around the property at close range identifies any missed spots, drip marks, or smears before equipment is packed away. Corrections are made immediately. On lakefront or south-facing properties where glare can obscure streaks during the clean itself, this inspection step is done from multiple angles to catch anything that wasn't visible head-on.

What sets us apart: Exterior-only cleans with Wagon Windows use the same professional tools and sequencing as a full clean — not a shortcut version — so the result is identical glass quality without the interior access required. It's the fastest way to transform a home's curb appeal in the Shuswap in a single visit.

Common Exterior Problems We Solve in Sicamous

  • Cottonwood fluff embeds in exterior frames and sills every May and June, blocking the clean and contaminating solutions if not removed first
  • South-facing exterior windows bake on dust and UV haze from Salmon Arm's long sun exposure, creating a dull film that standard wiping won't clear
  • Irrigation sprinkler overspray deposits hard minerals on windows adjacent to lawns and garden beds, building up into white calcium rings
  • Wildfire smoke from Interior BC fire seasons coats glass with an oily residue that smears if not treated with the right emulsifying solution
  • Winter road salt spray on windows near driveways and streets leaves persistent white residue that worsens with each rain cycle

Once the exterior clean is done, every outward-facing pane is clear, streak-free, and reflective — the difference is visible from the street immediately. Frames and sills look clean rather than grimy, which changes the overall impression of the property more than most people expect. Natural light enters rooms noticeably brighter because there is no haze layer filtering it.

When to Book Exterior Window Cleaning in Sicamous

Most Sicamous properties benefit from exterior 2–3 times per year: spring after pollen peaks (April–May), midsummer for dust and UV haze (July), and fall before freeze (September–October). Lakefront properties may need an additional clean in late spring or early summer due to higher mineral spray exposure from the water.

Sicamous sits between two lakes — windborne moisture and mineral spray are significant. Higher tourism traffic in summer means commercial cleaning demand peaks sharply in June–August.

Booking lead time for Sicamous: Because Sicamous is approximately 45 minutes from our Salmon Arm base, we schedule Sicamous jobs in batches when possible. We recommend booking at least 1–2 weeks in advance, especially during spring and fall peak periods.

To lock in your preferred date, book online via Calendly or call us at (403) 837-5092. We'll send a confirmation within a few hours.

Frequently Asked Questions — Exterior in Sicamous

Does Wagon Windows clean exterior windows on Sicamous properties adjacent to the marina district?

Yes — marina-adjacent properties in Sicamous deal with diesel exhaust residue and water spray on top of the standard lake mineral deposits. This combination creates a greasy-mineral film on exterior glass that requires a degreaser pre-treatment before the mineral rinse and main squeegee pass. We carry the appropriate products for marina-adjacent properties and build the extra step into the quote. Standard exterior pricing starts at $175 for Sicamous residential — marina-adjacent properties with contamination may run slightly higher.

My Sicamous home faces Mara Lake — does exterior glass on Mara Lake face different conditions than Shuswap Lake properties?

Mara Lake's mineral content is similar to Shuswap Lake, so the calcium and silica deposit challenge is essentially the same. What's different is the wind direction — Mara Lake properties often catch a different prevailing wind than Shuswap-facing homes, meaning the spray pattern on the glass can be more concentrated on specific window faces. We assess which elevation and orientation gets the worst accumulation on the initial visit and allocate extra time accordingly. Mara-facing glass usually needs the same 3-clean annual schedule as Shuswap waterfront.

Can you set up a recurring exterior window cleaning schedule for my Sicamous home before tourist season?

Yes — a recurring annual exterior schedule works well for Sicamous homeowners. We set up a May pre-season appointment and a September post-summer appointment as the minimum, with an optional August mid-season visit for properties with heavy lake exposure. Once you're in our system, we reach out in late winter to confirm your dates for the coming year. Summer availability in Sicamous fills fast, so recurring customers get priority scheduling over new single-visit bookings during peak months.

How does the higher summer tourism traffic in Sicamous affect exterior window cleaning timing and availability?

Summer demand in Sicamous from June through August is the highest of any location in our service area. The combination of vacation rental operators, residential homeowners, and commercial businesses all booking pre-season means May availability fills by April. For exterior cleaning specifically, we recommend contacting us by March for a May appointment in Sicamous. If you miss the pre-season window, we have limited July and August availability but scheduling flexibility is reduced. September is reliably available and still effective before freeze.

What does an exterior window cleaning include?

Our exterior clean covers all accessible exterior-facing windows, a squeegee + detail finish for streak-free results, sill and frame wipe-down, and any hard-to-reach windows using extension equipment.

Can you add interior cleaning later if I start with exterior only?

Absolutely — many customers start with exterior-only to see the results and add interior cleaning on a follow-up visit. Just let us know when booking.

Does Wagon Windows serve Sicamous?

Yes — Sicamous is within our service area, approximately 45 minutes from Salmon Arm. We serve residential homes, vacation rentals, and commercial businesses in Sicamous. Summer is peak season — book early.

Do you clean commercial windows for Sicamous businesses during tourist season?

Yes — we work with Sicamous businesses including storefronts, restaurants, and accommodation operators. Summer pre-season cleans (May) are high demand — book your commercial clean by April to guarantee availability.

Do Sicamous vacation rental properties need gutter cleaning before tourist season?

Yes — pre-season gutter cleaning in April or May is worth doing on Sicamous vacation rentals. Pine debris from the surrounding forests and winter storm material pack into gutters over the off-season. Blocked gutters that overflow during summer storms create fascia staining visible to guests and can damage the structure of rental-income properties.

What areas does Wagon Windows serve?

Wagon Windows serves the entire Shuswap region from our Salmon Arm base: Salmon Arm, Sorrento, Tappen, Blind Bay, Sicamous, Chase, Enderby, and Armstrong. Contact us if your area isn't listed — we travel for the right job.

How do I get a quote from Wagon Windows?

The fastest way is to book a free 30-minute consultation via our Calendly link at wagonwindows.com — you'll have a confirmed appointment within minutes. You can also call or text (403) 837-5092, or email davidhanna@wagonwindow.com. We respond within a few hours.

Is Wagon Windows insured?

Yes — Wagon Windows carries liability insurance. We treat your property with care and take full responsibility for our work on every job.

What is the minimum job fee?

Our minimum job fee is $99. This covers smaller jobs, single-room requests, and add-ons like screen cleaning or track cleaning on their own.

Do you use eco-friendly products?

Yes — we use biodegradable, streak-free cleaning solutions that are safe for gardens, pets, and the Shuswap's waterways. We don't use harsh chemicals that could harm your landscaping or nearby water.
